response:
To compute the **sample standard deviation** of the data set:

$$
\{-11, 9, 5, 15, -10, -15, 12, -25, -22\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the mean (average) of the data

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{-11 + 9 + 5 + 15 - 10 - 15 + 12 - 25 - 22}{9}
$$

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{-11 + 9 = -2, -2 + 5 = 3, 3 + 15 = 18, 18 - 10 = 8, 8 - 15 = -7, -7 + 12 = 5, 5 - 25 = -20, -20 - 22 = -42}{9}
$$

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{-42}{9} = -4.666\ldots = -\frac{42}{9} = -\frac{14}{3}
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the squared differences from the mean

$$
\begin{align*}
(-11 - (-14/3))^2 &= \left(-11 + \frac{14}{3}\right)^2 = \left(-\frac{33}{3} + \frac{14}{3}\right)^2 = \left(-\frac{19}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{361}{9} \\
(9 - (-14/3))^2 &= \left(9 + \frac{14}{3}\right)^2 = \left(\frac{27}{3} + \frac{14}{3}\right)^2 = \left(\frac{41}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{1681}{9} \\
(5 - (-14/3))^2 &= \left(5 + \frac{14}{3}\right)^2 = \left(\frac{15}{3} + \frac{14}{3}\right)^2 = \left(\frac{29}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{841}{
